title: The Vancouver Sun - September 30, 2017 (Page 98)
author: Unknown
card_summary: A newspaper clipping from The Vancouver Sun (Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada), dated September 30, 2017, page 98. This clipping postdates PKD's death by 35 years and may relate to continued cultural interest in his work, film or television adaptations, or retrospective coverage.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from The Vancouver Sun (Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada), dated Saturday, September 30, 2017, page 98. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 15, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

This September 2017 clipping coincides with the release of Blade Runner 2049 (October 6, 2017), the long-awaited sequel to the 1982 film based on Dick's novel Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep? Vancouver's connection to Dick through the 1972 convention and its role as a major film production center make it a natural site for coverage of the Blade Runner franchise. The timing strongly suggests this clipping relates to Blade Runner 2049 publicity or a retrospective on Dick's work.
